Construcción de SummarizedExperiment – GSE94873 (NanoString sangre)

TFM Melanoma Inmunoterapia

1. Objetivo de este documento

El objetivo de este documento es construir un contenedor ómico tipo SummarizedExperiment para la cohorte GSE94873, integrando:

  • matriz de expresión NanoString,
  • metadatos clínicos de las muestras,
  • anotación de genes,
  • e información de preprocesamiento.

Características del dataset:

Campo Valor
Dataset GSE94873
Tejido Sangre periférica (PBMC / T cells)
Plataforma NanoString nCounter
Tratamiento Tremelimumab (anti-CTLA-4)
Rol en TFM Sensibilidad en sangre (bloque 03)

Nota: Los nombres exactos de columnas clínicas (response_col, baseline_col, sample_id_col) se obtienen de la exploración inicial (exploracion_inicial_GSE94873.Rmd). Si tras ejecutar la exploración alguna variable tiene un nombre diferente, actualizar la sección 7 antes de continuar.

2. Librerías necesarias

if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
  install.packages("BiocManager")

pkgs <- c(
  "GEOquery",
  "SummarizedExperiment",
  "S4Vectors",
  "dplyr",
  "tibble",
  "stringr"
)

for (p in pkgs) {
  if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly = TRUE)) {
    if (p %in% c("GEOquery", "SummarizedExperiment", "S4Vectors")) {
      BiocManager::install(p, ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
    } else {
      install.packages(p)
    }
  }
}
## Setting options('download.file.method.GEOquery'='auto')
## Setting options('GEOquery.inmemory.gpl'=FALSE)
library(GEOquery)
## Cargando paquete requerido: Biobase
## Cargando paquete requerido: BiocGenerics
## Cargando paquete requerido: generics
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'generics'

##     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank,
##     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, table, tapply, unique,
##     unsplit, which.max, which.min

##     Vignettes contain introductory material; view with

library(SummarizedExperiment)

3. Definición automática de rutas del proyecto

Este documento está pensado para ejecutarse desde la carpeta:

TFM/scripts/GEO/GSE94873/

project_root    <- normalizePath(file.path("..", "..", ".."), winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
raw_base_dir    <- file.path(project_root, "GEO_raw")
raw_dataset_dir <- file.path(raw_base_dir, "GSE94873")
se_dir          <- file.path(project_root, "SummarizedExperiment_objects")

dir.create(raw_base_dir,    recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(raw_dataset_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(se_dir,          recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

project_root
## [1] "C:/Users/sonia/Desktop/2_TFM_MELANOMA"
raw_base_dir
## [1] "C:/Users/sonia/Desktop/2_TFM_MELANOMA/GEO_raw"
raw_dataset_dir
## [1] "C:/Users/sonia/Desktop/2_TFM_MELANOMA/GEO_raw/GSE94873"
se_dir
## [1] "C:/Users/sonia/Desktop/2_TFM_MELANOMA/SummarizedExperiment_objects"

4. Descarga de metadatos y matriz de expresión GEO

gse94873_list <- getGEO("GSE94873", GSEMatrix = TRUE)
## Found 1 file(s)
## GSE94873_series_matrix.txt.gz
length(gse94873_list)
## [1] 1
gse94873_eset <- gse94873_list[[1]]

expr_matrix  <- Biobase::exprs(gse94873_eset)
metadata_geo <- Biobase::pData(gse94873_eset)
gene_info    <- Biobase::fData(gse94873_eset)

dim(expr_matrix)
## [1] 169 720
dim(metadata_geo)
## [1] 720  46
dim(gene_info)
## [1] 169   5

5. Guardado de metadatos crudos

raw_metadata_file <- file.path(raw_dataset_dir, "GSE94873_metadata_raw.tsv")
write.table(metadata_geo, raw_metadata_file, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = TRUE)

raw_metadata_file
## [1] "C:/Users/sonia/Desktop/2_TFM_MELANOMA/GEO_raw/GSE94873/GSE94873_metadata_raw.tsv"

6. Comprobación de la matriz de expresión

# Si la matriz está vacía, puede requerirse archivo suplementario
if (nrow(expr_matrix) == 0) {
  stop(paste(
    "La matriz de expresión del ExpressionSet está vacía.",
    "Consultar exploracion_inicial_GSE94873.Rmd para identificar el archivo suplementario correcto",
    "y adaptar la carga de la matriz en la siguiente sección."
  ))
}

cat("Dimensiones de la matriz:", dim(expr_matrix), "\n")
## Dimensiones de la matriz: 169 720
cat("Rango de valores:\n")
## Rango de valores:
print(summary(as.vector(expr_matrix)))
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
##    0.00   15.89   17.78   18.29   20.58   26.47      18

7. Definición de columnas clave

IMPORTANTE: Actualizar estos valores si la exploración inicial revela nombres de columna distintos. Ejecutar primero exploracion_inicial_GSE94873.Rmd y consultar la sección de overlap de identificadores y las columnas candidatas de respuesta/baseline.

sample_id_col <- "geo_accession"
response_col  <- "immunotherapy response (responder=1 nonresponder=0):ch1"
baseline_col  <- "source_name_ch1"
treatment_col <- NA_character_
treatment_label <- "Tremelimumab (anti-CTLA-4)"

cat("sample_id_col:", sample_id_col, "\n")
## sample_id_col: geo_accession
cat("response_col: ", response_col,  "\n")
## response_col:  immunotherapy response (responder=1 nonresponder=0):ch1
cat("baseline_col: ", baseline_col,  "\n")
## baseline_col:  source_name_ch1
cat("treatment_col:", treatment_col, "\n")
## treatment_col: NA
cat("treatment_label:", treatment_label, "\n")
## treatment_label: Tremelimumab (anti-CTLA-4)
# Verificar que sample_id_col existe
if (!sample_id_col %in% colnames(metadata_geo)) {
  stop("sample_id_col '", sample_id_col, "' no encontrada. Revisar exploración inicial.")
}
if (!is.na(response_col) && !response_col %in% colnames(metadata_geo)) {
  stop("response_col '", response_col, "' no encontrada. Revisar exploración inicial.")
}
if (!is.na(baseline_col) && !baseline_col %in% colnames(metadata_geo)) {
  stop("baseline_col '", baseline_col, "' no encontrada. Revisar exploración inicial.")
}

8. Funciones auxiliares

8.1. Recodificación de respuesta binaria

make_response_binary <- function(x) {
  x_clean <- stringr::str_to_upper(trimws(as.character(x)))

  dplyr::case_when(
    x_clean %in% c("1", "1.0") ~ "Responder",
    x_clean %in% c("0", "0.0") ~ "No_responder",
    x_clean %in% c("CR", "PR", "COMPLETE RESPONSE", "PARTIAL RESPONSE",
                   "PRCR", "R", "RESPONSE", "RESPONDER", "YES", "BENEFIT") ~ "Responder",
    x_clean %in% c("SD", "PD", "STABLE DISEASE", "PROGRESSIVE DISEASE",
                   "NR", "NON-RESPONSE", "NON RESPONDER", "NONRESPONDER",
                   "NO RESPONSE", "NO", "NO BENEFIT")                       ~ "No_responder",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}

8.2. Detección de pretratamiento / baseline

is_baseline_sample <- function(x) {
  x_clean <- stringr::str_to_lower(trimws(as.character(x)))

  dplyr::case_when(
    stringr::str_detect(x_clean, "baseline|pretreatment|pre-treatment|pre treatment|^pre$|before treatment|^c1d1$|week 0|^0$|wk0") ~ TRUE,
    stringr::str_detect(x_clean, "on.?treatment|on.?tx|post|week [1-9]|cycle [2-9]|wk[1-9]")                                        ~ FALSE,
    TRUE ~ NA
  )
}

9. Construcción de metadatos clínicos

metadata_clean <- metadata_geo

metadata_clean$sample_id  <- trimws(as.character(metadata_clean[[sample_id_col]]))
metadata_clean$patient_id <- stringr::str_remove(trimws(as.character(metadata_clean$title)), "^(Pre|Post)\\s+")

# Respuesta
if (!is.na(response_col)) {
  metadata_clean$response_raw    <- metadata_clean[[response_col]]
  metadata_clean$response_binary <- make_response_binary(metadata_clean$response_raw)
} else {
  warning("response_col no definida. Añadir respuesta manualmente o actualizar la sección 7.")
  metadata_clean$response_raw    <- NA_character_
  metadata_clean$response_binary <- NA_character_
}

# Baseline
if (!is.na(baseline_col)) {
  metadata_clean$is_baseline <- is_baseline_sample(metadata_clean[[baseline_col]])
} else {
  # Sin variable de timepoint: se asumen todas las muestras como pretratamiento
  # (verificar con la exploración inicial)
  metadata_clean$is_baseline <- TRUE
  message("baseline_col no definida. Se asumen todas las muestras como pretratamiento.")
}

# Tratamiento
if (!is.na(treatment_col) && treatment_col %in% colnames(metadata_clean)) {
  metadata_clean$treatment <- metadata_clean[[treatment_col]]
} else {
  metadata_clean$treatment <- treatment_label
}

# Variables fijas del dataset
metadata_clean$dataset  <- "GSE94873"
metadata_clean$tissue   <- "Blood"
metadata_clean$platform <- "NanoString"
metadata_clean$timepoint_raw <- metadata_clean[[baseline_col]]
metadata_clean$sample_title  <- metadata_clean$title

metadata_clean
##                    title geo_accession                status submission_date

##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   label_protocol_ch1
## GSM2486822 First-strand complementary DNA was synthesized from random hexamer-primed RNA templates using TaqMan® reverse-transcription reagents. Synthesis of first strand cDNA for use in PCR amplification used Applied Biosystems TAQMAN Reverse Transcription Reagents Kit (P/N 808-0234). Individual target-gene amplification was multiplexed with the 18S rRNA endogenous control and run in triplicate in 384-well format on the 7900HT fast real-time PCR system.  PCR procedures followed ABI Prism® 7900 Sequence Detection System instructions. Applied Biosystems (Foster City, Calif.)
## GSM2486823 First-strand complementary DNA was synthesized from random hexamer-primed RNA templates using TaqMan® reverse-transcription reagents. Synthesis of first strand cDNA for use in PCR amplification used Applied Biosystems TAQMAN Reverse Transcription Reagents Kit (P/N 808-0234). Individual target-gene amplification was multiplexed with the 18S rRNA endogenous control and run in triplicate in 384-well format on the 7900HT fast real-time PCR system.  PCR procedures followed ABI Prism® 7900 Sequence Detection System instructions. Applied Biosystems (Foster City, Calif.)

PCR procedures followed ABI Prism® 7900 Sequence Detection System instructions. Applied Biosystems (Foster City, Calif.)

Individual target-gene amplification was multiplexed with the 18S rRNA endogenous control and run in triplicate in 384-well format on the 7900HT fast real-time PCR system.  PCR procedures followed ABI Prism® 7900 Sequence Detection System instructions. Applied Biosystems (Foster City, Calif.)

Synthesis of first strand cDNA for use in PCR amplification used Applied Biosystems TAQMAN Reverse Transcription Reagents Kit (P/N 808-0234). Synthesis of first strand cDNA for use in PCR amplification used Applied Biosystems TAQMAN Reverse Transcription Reagents Kit (P/N 808-0234). Synthesis of first strand cDNA for use in PCR amplification used Applied Biosystems TAQMAN Reverse Transcription Reagents Kit (P/N 808-0234). Individual target-gene amplification was multiplexed with the 18S rRNA endogenous control and run in triplicate in 384-well format on the 7900HT fast real-time PCR system.  PCR procedures followed ABI Prism® 7900 Sequence Detection System instructions. Applied Biosystems (Foster City, Calif.)

## GSM2487541 Phase IIb biomarker study, Advanced Melanoma, 15 mg/kg dose, previous chemotherapy
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   data_processing
## GSM2486822 The normalized data is reported in delta CT units. 18S rRNA is the sole endogenous control gene. With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.

If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.
## GSM2487541 The normalized data is reported in delta CT units. 18S rRNA is the sole endogenous control gene. With each heating/cooling cycle, the real time PCR assay detects fluorescent signal accumulation. The Ct (cycle threshold) is the number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to exceed background level. Thermal cycling goes through 40 cycles, and 18S rRNA is measured at the 14th cycle. Delta Ct is measured up to 26 cycles from the point endogenous control is recognized. Raw CT data is automatically processed to delta CT data by a software program subtracting the 18S CT value from the raw data CT value for each triplicate well. Triplicate test results are used to identify and eliminate data points that are statistical “outliers”; such data points are those that differ by a percentage greater than 3% of the average of all three values. If more than one data point in a set of three is excluded by this procedure, then all data for the relevant constituent is discarded.
##                                                                                                                                                 data_processing.1

10. Comprobación de variables clínicas

cat("\nrespuesta raw:\n");    print(table(metadata_clean$response_raw,    useNA = "ifany"))
## 
## respuesta raw:
## 
##   0   1 
## 626  94
cat("\nrespuesta binaria:\n"); print(table(metadata_clean$response_binary, useNA = "ifany"))
## 
## respuesta binaria:
## 
## No_responder    Responder 
##          626           94
cat("\nis_baseline:\n");       print(table(metadata_clean$is_baseline,     useNA = "ifany"))
## 
## is_baseline:
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
##   360   360
cat("\ntratamiento:\n");       print(table(metadata_clean$treatment,        useNA = "ifany"))
## 
## tratamiento:
## 
## Tremelimumab (anti-CTLA-4) 
##                        720

11. Filtrado a muestras pretratamiento

Solo se retienen muestras pretratamiento para el análisis predictivo.

n_antes <- nrow(metadata_clean)

metadata_clean <- metadata_clean %>%
  dplyr::filter(is_baseline %in% TRUE)

n_despues <- nrow(metadata_clean)

cat("Muestras antes del filtrado:", n_antes, "\n")
## Muestras antes del filtrado: 720
cat("Muestras pretratamiento retenidas:", n_despues, "\n")
## Muestras pretratamiento retenidas: 360
if (n_despues == 0) {
  stop("No quedan muestras tras el filtrado pretratamiento. Revisar baseline_col en la sección 7.")
}

cat("\nDistribución de respuesta en muestras pretratamiento:\n")
## 
## Distribución de respuesta en muestras pretratamiento:
print(table(metadata_clean$response_binary, useNA = "ifany"))
## 
## No_responder    Responder 
##          314           46

12. Alineamiento entre expresión y metadatos

colnames(expr_matrix)      <- trimws(colnames(expr_matrix))
metadata_clean$sample_id   <- trimws(as.character(metadata_clean$sample_id))

expr_only <- setdiff(colnames(expr_matrix), metadata_clean$sample_id)
meta_only <- setdiff(metadata_clean$sample_id, colnames(expr_matrix))

cat("Muestras solo en expresión:", length(expr_only), "\n")
## Muestras solo en expresión: 360
cat("Muestras solo en metadatos:", length(meta_only), "\n")
## Muestras solo en metadatos: 0
if (length(expr_only) > 0) cat("Ejemplos:", paste(head(expr_only, 5), collapse = ", "), "\n")
## Ejemplos: GSM2487032, GSM2487033, GSM2487034, GSM2487035, GSM2487036
if (length(meta_only) > 0) cat("Ejemplos:", paste(head(meta_only, 5), collapse = ", "), "\n")

common_samples <- intersect(colnames(expr_matrix), metadata_clean$sample_id)

if (length(common_samples) == 0) {
  stop("No hay muestras comunes entre la matriz de expresión y los metadatos. Revisar sample_id_col.")
}

expr_matrix    <- expr_matrix[, common_samples, drop = FALSE]
metadata_clean <- metadata_clean %>% dplyr::filter(sample_id %in% common_samples)
rownames(metadata_clean) <- metadata_clean$sample_id
metadata_clean <- metadata_clean[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]

cat("\nMuestras comunes finales:", length(common_samples), "\n")
## 
## Muestras comunes finales: 360
dim(expr_matrix)
## [1] 169 360

13. Anotacion genica

En GSE94873, los rownames originales de la matriz son identificadores del panel NanoString (A1, A10, etc.). El simbolo genico esta en la columna ORF del fData; por tanto, se usa ORF como gene_symbol y como rowname final para facilitar la integracion con el resto del proyecto.

gene_annotation <- as.data.frame(gene_info)
gene_annotation$probe_id <- rownames(expr_matrix)

if ("ORF" %in% colnames(gene_annotation)) {
  gene_annotation$gene_symbol <- toupper(trimws(as.character(gene_annotation$ORF)))
} else if ("gene_symbol" %in% colnames(gene_annotation)) {
  gene_annotation$gene_symbol <- toupper(trimws(as.character(gene_annotation$gene_symbol)))
} else {
  gene_annotation$gene_symbol <- toupper(trimws(rownames(expr_matrix)))
}

if (anyDuplicated(gene_annotation$gene_symbol)) {
  duplicated_symbols <- unique(gene_annotation$gene_symbol[duplicated(gene_annotation$gene_symbol)])
  stop("Gene symbols duplicados en GSE94873: ", paste(duplicated_symbols, collapse = ", "))
}

rownames(expr_matrix) <- gene_annotation$gene_symbol
rownames(gene_annotation) <- gene_annotation$gene_symbol

head(gene_annotation)
##          ID    ORF                                    Name
## ADAM17   A1 ADAM17         ADAM Metallopeptidase Domain 17
## BRCA1   A10  BRCA1            Breast Cancer 1, Early Onset
## LGALS3 A100 LGALS3 Lectin, Galactoside-Binding, Soluble, 3
## LTA    A101    LTA                       Lymphotoxin Alpha
## MAPK14 A102 MAPK14     Mitogen-Activated Protein Kinase 14
## MCAM   A103   MCAM         Melanoma Cell Adhesion Molecule
##                                                                                 Aliases
## ADAM17                                        ADAM18, CD156B, NISBD1, NISBD, TACE, CSVP
## BRCA1                   BRCC1, BRCAI, FANCS, IRIS, PPP1R53, BROVCA1, PNCA4, PSCP, RNF53
## LGALS3            L31, CBP35, GAL3, GALIG, galectin-3, LGALS2, GALBP, MAC2, Gal-3, L-31
## LTA                             LT, lymphotoxin-alpha, TNFB, LT-alpha, TNF-beta, TNFSF1
## MAPK14 P38, RK, CSBP, EXIP, Mxi2, PRKM14, PRKM15, p38ALPHA, CSBP2, CSPB1, CSBP1, SAPK2A
## MCAM                                                              Gicerin, CD146, MUC18
##                                                             URL probe_id
## ADAM17 http://www.genecards.org/cgi-bin/carddisp.pl?gene=ADAM17       A1
## BRCA1   http://www.genecards.org/cgi-bin/carddisp.pl?gene=BRCA1      A10
## LGALS3 http://www.genecards.org/cgi-bin/carddisp.pl?gene=LGALS3     A100
## LTA       http://www.genecards.org/cgi-bin/carddisp.pl?gene=LTA     A101
## MAPK14 http://www.genecards.org/cgi-bin/carddisp.pl?gene=MAPK14     A102
## MCAM     http://www.genecards.org/cgi-bin/carddisp.pl?gene=MCAM     A103
##        gene_symbol
## ADAM17      ADAM17
## BRCA1        BRCA1
## LGALS3      LGALS3
## LTA            LTA
## MAPK14      MAPK14
## MCAM          MCAM
cat("\nTotal genes:", nrow(gene_annotation), "\n")
## 
## Total genes: 169

14. Selección final de columnas clínicas

cols_keep <- c("sample_id", "patient_id", "sample_title", "response_raw", "response_binary",
               "is_baseline", "timepoint_raw", "treatment", "dataset", "tissue", "platform",
               "age:ch1", "gender:ch1", "cancer stage:ch1",
               "survival status 12 months (1=alive 0=dead):ch1",
               "worst diarrhea grade:ch1")

metadata_clean <- metadata_clean %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(cols_keep))

metadata_clean

15. Verificación previa

stopifnot(identical(colnames(expr_matrix), rownames(metadata_clean)))
stopifnot(identical(rownames(expr_matrix), rownames(gene_annotation)))

cat("Verificación superada: expresión, metadatos y anotación alineados.\n")
## Verificación superada: expresión, metadatos y anotación alineados.

16. Construcción del SummarizedExperiment

se_gse94873 <- SummarizedExperiment(
  assays  = list(expr = expr_matrix),
  colData = S4Vectors::DataFrame(metadata_clean),
  rowData = S4Vectors::DataFrame(gene_annotation)
)

se_gse94873
## class: SummarizedExperiment 
## dim: 169 360 
## metadata(0):
## assays(1): expr
## rownames(169): ADAM17 BRCA1 ... LARGE LCK
## rowData names(7): ID ORF ... probe_id gene_symbol
## colnames(360): GSM2486822 GSM2486823 ... GSM2487390 GSM2487391
## colData names(16): sample_id patient_id ...
##   survival.status.12.months..1.alive.0.dead..ch1
##   worst.diarrhea.grade.ch1

17. Metadatos del preprocesamiento

metadata(se_gse94873)$dataset            <- "GSE94873"
metadata(se_gse94873)$description        <- "Melanoma NanoString blood cohort, tremelimumab anti-CTLA-4"
metadata(se_gse94873)$expression_units   <- "NanoString normalized expression"
metadata(se_gse94873)$tissue             <- "Blood"
metadata(se_gse94873)$treatment          <- treatment_label
metadata(se_gse94873)$sample_id_column   <- sample_id_col
metadata(se_gse94873)$response_column    <- response_col
metadata(se_gse94873)$baseline_column    <- baseline_col
metadata(se_gse94873)$treatment_column   <- treatment_col
metadata(se_gse94873)$gene_identifier    <- "gene_symbol (NanoString panel)"
metadata(se_gse94873)$preprocessing      <- paste(
  "Expression extracted from GEO ExpressionSet;",
  "response recoded from immunotherapy response 1/0;",
  "pre-treatment samples only retained."
)
metadata(se_gse94873)$date_created       <- Sys.Date()

18. Guardado del contenedor

se_output_file <- file.path(se_dir, "se_gse94873.rds")
saveRDS(se_gse94873, se_output_file)

cat("Objeto guardado en:", se_output_file, "\n")
## Objeto guardado en: C:/Users/sonia/Desktop/2_TFM_MELANOMA/SummarizedExperiment_objects/se_gse94873.rds
file.exists(se_output_file)
## [1] TRUE

19. Checklist específica de GSE94873

checklist_gse94873 <- tibble::tibble(
  paso = c(
    "ExpressionSet descargado",
    "Matriz de expresión extraída",
    "Metadatos clínicos extraídos",
    "sample_id definido y alineado",
    "Respuesta identificada y recodificada",
    "Baseline identificado / todas muestras pretratamiento",
    "Muestras pretratamiento filtradas",
    "Muestras alineadas expresion-metadatos",
    "Anotacion genica asignada",
    "SummarizedExperiment construido",
    "Objeto guardado en SummarizedExperiment_objects/"
  ),
  estado = c(
    "Si",
    ifelse(nrow(expr_matrix) > 0, "Si", "No"),
    ifelse(ncol(metadata_clean) > 0, "Si", "No"),
    ifelse(sample_id_col %in% colnames(metadata_geo), "Si", "No - revisar"),
    ifelse(!is.na(response_col) && sum(!is.na(metadata_clean$response_binary)) > 0, "Si", "Pendiente - actualizar response_col"),
    ifelse(!is.na(baseline_col) || all(metadata_clean$is_baseline %in% TRUE), "Si", "Pendiente - actualizar baseline_col"),
    ifelse(nrow(metadata_clean) > 0, paste("Si -", nrow(metadata_clean), "muestras"), "No"),
    ifelse(identical(colnames(expr_matrix), rownames(metadata_clean)), "Si", "No"),
    ifelse("gene_symbol" %in% colnames(gene_annotation), "Si", "No"),
    ifelse(inherits(se_gse94873, "SummarizedExperiment"), "Si", "No"),
    ifelse(file.exists(se_output_file), "Si", "No")
  )
)

print(checklist_gse94873, n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 11 × 2
##    paso                                                  estado           
##    <chr>                                                 <chr>            
##  1 ExpressionSet descargado                              Si               
##  2 Matriz de expresión extraída                          Si               
##  3 Metadatos clínicos extraídos                          Si               
##  4 sample_id definido y alineado                         Si               
##  5 Respuesta identificada y recodificada                 Si               
##  6 Baseline identificado / todas muestras pretratamiento Si               
##  7 Muestras pretratamiento filtradas                     Si - 360 muestras
##  8 Muestras alineadas expresion-metadatos                Si               
##  9 Anotacion genica asignada                             Si               
## 10 SummarizedExperiment construido                       Si               
## 11 Objeto guardado en SummarizedExperiment_objects/      Si

20. Conclusión

Con este documento se construye el SummarizedExperiment de GSE94873 siguiendo el mismo pipeline que el resto de cohortes del proyecto:

  • Tejido: sangre periférica (PBMC)
  • Plataforma: NanoString
  • Tratamiento: Tremelimumab (anti-CTLA-4)
  • Muestras: solo pretratamiento
  • Genes: en formato gene symbol

El objeto resultante (se_gse94873.rds) queda en SummarizedExperiment_objects/ y puede incorporarse directamente al bloque de sensibilidad en sangre (03_blood_sensitivity.Rmd).
